question: What are the items you must sign in at the end of your shift?
options: Pillows, duvet and duvet cover
Keycard, room attendant's report and walkie talkie
Guests' lost and found items
Dental kit, sanitary bag and soap
correctAnswer: Keycard, room attendant's report and walkie talkie
hint: Think about what you were given at the START of your shift that belongs to the hotel.
explanation: At the end of your shift, you sign in the keycard, room attendant's report and walkie talkie. These are hotel equipment issued to you at the start of your shift and must be returned.

question: Are you allowed to help your colleagues sign out for their work items?
options: Yes, it is important to be helpful.
Yes, as long as my supervisor does not know about it.
No, my colleagues did not help me sign out for my work items.
No, it is against company rules to sign out work items for colleagues.
correctAnswer: No, it is against company rules to sign out work items for colleagues.
hint: Each person must be accountable for their own items. Signing out for someone else is not allowed.
explanation: It is against company rules to sign out work items for colleagues. Every staff member must personally sign out their own items so they are accountable for them.

question: What are the items required for cleaning and servicing guestrooms and serviced apartments?
options: A) Cleaning chemicals in a caddy, B) Walkie talkie and C) Dirty towels
A) Cleaning chemicals in a caddy, C) Dirty towels, D) Cleaning cloths and E) Rubber gloves
A) Cleaning chemicals in a caddy, D) Cleaning cloths, E) Rubber gloves and F) New guest supplies
All of the above
correctAnswer: A) Cleaning chemicals in a caddy, D) Cleaning cloths, E) Rubber gloves and F) New guest supplies
hint: You need cleaning chemicals, cloths for wiping, gloves to protect your hands, and fresh supplies for the guest. A walkie talkie is for communication, not cleaning.
explanation: The correct items are: A) Cleaning chemicals in a caddy, D) Cleaning cloths, E) Rubber gloves, and F) New guest supplies. A walkie talkie (B) is communication equipment and dirty towels (C) are collected from rooms — neither are cleaning tools you bring to service a room.

question: What will you do when you see a "Do Not Disturb" sign?
options: Press the doorbell.
Wait for the guest to open the door.
Enter the room and wake the guest up.
Go to the next room shown on your room attendant's report.
correctAnswer: Go to the next room shown on your room attendant's report.
hint: The DND sign means the guest does NOT want to be disturbed at all. What do you do instead?
explanation: When you see a 'Do Not Disturb' sign, respect the guest's privacy and go to the next room on your room attendant's report. Never press the doorbell, wait, or enter a room with a DND sign.

question: It is past the guest's check out time of 3pm. What will you do if the "Do Not Disturb" sign is still hanging on the door?
options: Inform your supervisor.
Press the doorbell and inform the guest to check out.
Press the doorbell and scold the guest for not checking out.
Go to the next room shown on your room attendant's report.
correctAnswer: Inform your supervisor.
hint: Check out time has passed — this is an unusual situation. You should not handle this alone. Who do you report to?
explanation: When check out time has passed but the DND sign is still up, inform your supervisor immediately. Do not scold the guest or handle this situation on your own — your supervisor needs to decide what to do.

question: Which photo shows a clean and well-presented room?
options: A dark room with a dishevelled, unmade bed
A neatly made bed with smooth sheets, pillows in place and bedside lights on
A messy bed with sheets and blankets hanging off the sides
A bed that has not been made up, with items left on it
correctAnswer: A neatly made bed with smooth sheets, pillows in place and bedside lights on
hint: A well-presented room looks neat, tidy, and ready for the guest.
explanation: A clean and well-presented room has a neatly made bed with smooth sheets, pillows properly placed, and the room looking tidy and welcoming for the guest.

question: Which statement is CORRECT?
options: A clean and well-presented room makes guests feel happy, comfortable and safe.
A clean and well-presented room attracts more guests to stay in the hotel or serviced apartment.
A clean and well-presented room shows that staff are professional and are doing a good job.
All of the above.
correctAnswer: All of the above.
hint: Think about all the people who benefit from a clean room — the guest, the hotel's business, and the staff.
explanation: All three statements are correct. A clean room makes guests feel happy, comfortable and safe; attracts more guests to the hotel or serviced apartment; and shows staff are professional and doing a good job.

question: Which TWO (2) REASONS will give guests a good impression of you?
options: Thick and colourful makeup
Clean and neat uniform
Dirty fingernails
Clean shaven
correctAnswer: Clean and neat uniform
Clean shaven
hint: A professional appearance is neat and clean. Heavy makeup and dirty nails do not create a good impression.
explanation: A clean and neat uniform and being clean shaven give guests a good impression. Thick colourful makeup and dirty fingernails are unprofessional and should be avoided at work.

question: Which set of photos show standard beds in a hotel?
options: Set 1: A regular hotel bed and a bunk bed
Set 2: Twin beds and a double bed in a hotel room
Set 3: A floor mattress and a sofa bed
Set 4: Japanese-style floor beds and a small single bed
correctAnswer: Set 2: Twin beds and a double bed in a hotel room
hint: Standard hotel beds are proper beds with frames and mattresses. Bunk beds, sofa beds, and floor mats are not standard hotel room beds.
explanation: Standard hotel beds are twin beds (two single beds) and double beds (one large bed). Bunk beds, sofa beds, floor mattresses, and Japanese-style floor beds are not standard hotel room bed types.

question: If a guest left the keycard in the room, I can ___.
options: help the guest by opening the door to the room
give the guest my keycard to open the door to the room
politely tell the guest I cannot open the door because I am very busy with work
politely tell the guest I cannot open the door because I need to protect the guest's safety and security
correctAnswer: politely tell the guest I cannot open the door because I need to protect the guest's safety and security
hint: You cannot verify who this person really is. Opening the door for an unverified person is a safety risk.
explanation: You must politely tell the guest you cannot open the door because you need to protect the guest's safety and security. You have no way to confirm the person's identity — direct them to the front desk instead.

question: I am NOT allowed to open the door for the guest because I ___.
options: am not a friend of the guest
did not seek permission from my supervisor
am not able to open the door with my keycard
do not have the guest's information to confirm his / her identity
correctAnswer: do not have the guest's information to confirm his / her identity
hint: The reason is about safety — you cannot prove the person is actually the guest who booked that room.
explanation: You are not allowed to open the door because you do not have the guest's information to confirm his / her identity. Anyone could claim to be the guest. Direct them to the front desk to verify their identity.

question: If a guest needs assistance to open their room door, I should direct the guest to the ___.
options: bellboy
front desk
restaurant manager
supervisor
correctAnswer: front desk
hint: Who has the guest's booking information and can verify their identity to give them access?
explanation: Direct the guest to the front desk. The front desk has the guest's booking information and can verify their identity before granting access to the room.

question: What is the safety posture when making the bed?
options: Sit on the bed
Kneel on the bed
Kneel on the carpet
Stand and bend over the bed
correctAnswer: Kneel on the carpet
hint: Think about which posture protects your back and keeps the bed clean.
explanation: Kneeling on the carpet is the correct safety posture when making the bed. It protects your back from injury and keeps the bed clean. Never sit or kneel on the bed itself as it dirties and damages it.

question: What is the CORRECT work sequence when cleaning guestrooms and serviced apartments?
options: Make bed → Pack caddy → Clean bathroom → Vacuum room → Dust room
Vacuum room → Make bed → Pack caddy → Dust room → Clean bathroom
Pack caddy → Clean bathroom → Dust room → Make bed → Vacuum room
Pack caddy → Make bed → Dust room → Clean bathroom → Vacuum room
correctAnswer: Pack caddy → Make bed → Dust room → Clean bathroom → Vacuum room
hint: Always prepare your tools first (pack caddy). Then work through the room. Vacuum LAST.
explanation: The correct sequence is: Pack caddy → Make bed → Dust room → Clean bathroom → Vacuum room. Pack the caddy first so you have all your tools. Vacuum last so you do not dirty the carpet again after cleaning.

question: It is important to vacuum the carpet LAST when servicing the guestrooms because ___ and ___.
options: my supervisors will be proud of me
my colleagues do not have to clean it again later
I will not dirty the carpet by performing other cleaning tasks
I should not walk and leave my footprints on the carpet after vacuuming
correctAnswer: I will not dirty the carpet by performing other cleaning tasks
I should not walk and leave my footprints on the carpet after vacuuming
hint: Think about what happens to a freshly vacuumed carpet if you keep working in the room or walk on it after.
explanation: Vacuum last for two reasons: (1) I will not dirty the carpet by performing other cleaning tasks after vacuuming, and (2) I should not walk and leave my footprints on the carpet after vacuuming. Always vacuum as your very last step.

question: To create a pleasant environment in the room during turndown service, I will ___ and ___.
options: switch on 1 bedside light
switch on both bedside lights
set the air-con temperature to 25°C
replace the used guest amenities
correctAnswer: switch on 1 bedside light
replace the used guest amenities
hint: Turndown service creates a soft, welcoming atmosphere. Think: gentle warm light, and fresh amenities for the guest.
explanation: For a pleasant turndown environment, switch on 1 bedside light (soft and relaxing — not both lights) and replace the used guest amenities so everything is fresh and ready for the guest.

question: What are TWO (2) REASONS for providing turndown service in hotels?
options: The guest can get ready for bed.
The room will be ready for the next guest to check in.
The guest will not need to make any request to the hotel.
The guest can relax and enjoy the evening before sleeping.
correctAnswer: The guest can get ready for bed.
The guest can relax and enjoy the evening before sleeping.
hint: Turndown service is done in the EVENING for the CURRENT guest. It is NOT for the next guest.
explanation: Turndown service is provided so the guest can get ready for bed and can relax and enjoy the evening before sleeping. It is not done to prepare the room for the next guest — that happens during regular servicing after checkout.

question: What is the LAST step when cleaning the kitchenette?
options: Wash the cutleries.
Empty the trash bin.
Sweep and mop the floor.
Wipe the countertop, induction cooker and refrigerator.
correctAnswer: Sweep and mop the floor.
hint: Just like vacuuming the carpet, what should you always do LAST in the kitchenette?
explanation: Sweep and mop the floor is the LAST step when cleaning the kitchenette. You do the floor last so any dirt from washing cutleries or wiping surfaces falls to the floor first — then you clean it.

question: What is the CORRECT work sequence when cleaning the kitchenette?
options: Wash cutleries → Wipe equipment and counters → Mop floor → Remove rubbish
Wash cutleries → Remove rubbish → Mop floor → Wipe equipment and counters
Remove rubbish → Mop floor → Wash cutleries → Wipe equipment and counters
Remove rubbish → Wash cutleries → Wipe equipment and counters → Mop floor
correctAnswer: Remove rubbish → Wash cutleries → Wipe equipment and counters → Mop floor
hint: Start by clearing the rubbish first. Then clean items and surfaces. Always mop the floor LAST.
explanation: The correct sequence is: Remove rubbish → Wash cutleries → Wipe equipment and counters → Mop floor. Clear waste first, then clean from top to bottom, and always mop the floor last.

question: Which TWO (2) statements are CORRECT? I have to ___ and ___.
options: keep the guest's information confidential
gossip about the guests with my colleagues
share the guest's medical conditions with my supervisor
protect the privacy of guests by not sharing guest information with other guests and colleagues
correctAnswer: keep the guest's information confidential
protect the privacy of guests by not sharing guest information with other guests and colleagues
hint: Guest information is completely private. A professional hotel worker never gossips or shares guest information.
explanation: You must keep the guest's information confidential AND protect the privacy of guests by not sharing guest information with other guests and colleagues. Never gossip about guests — it is unprofessional and violates their trust.

question: When we respect and protect the privacy of the guests, they will ___.
options: not come back to the hotel
trust the hotel and the staff
have their information leaked
not recommend the hotel to their friends to stay
correctAnswer: trust the hotel and the staff
hint: When guests feel their privacy is respected, how do they feel about the hotel?
explanation: When hotel staff respect and protect the privacy of guests, the guests will trust the hotel and the staff. This trust encourages guests to return and recommend the hotel to others.

question: Why is it important to seek feedback from my supervisors?
options: My fellow colleagues are doing so.
I can improve on skills that I did not do well.
I can build up more skills and become a professional employee.
I can tell my supervisors that I do not want to accept their feedback.
correctAnswer: I can improve on skills that I did not do well.
I can build up more skills and become a professional employee.
hint: Feedback helps you grow at work. Think about what YOU gain from receiving feedback from your supervisor.
explanation: It is important to seek feedback because I can improve on skills that I did not do well, and I can build up more skills and become a professional employee. Seeking feedback shows commitment to doing your job well.

question: When I seek feedback from my supervisors, I can ___ and ___.
options: learn from my supervisors which staff has resigned
learn how many newcomers joined the housekeeping team
learn to provide better and more professional services for my guests
gain more knowledge on housekeeping when supervisors share information with me
correctAnswer: learn to provide better and more professional services for my guests
gain more knowledge on housekeeping when supervisors share information with me
hint: Focus on what improves YOUR work and YOUR skills — not information about other staff members.
explanation: When I seek feedback from my supervisors, I can learn to provide better and more professional services for my guests, and gain more knowledge on housekeeping when supervisors share information with me.

question: How can I find out more about new cleaning tools and equipment to help me in my job?
options: Search the internet
Attend exhibitions and fairs
Read newspapers and magazines
All of the above
correctAnswer: All of the above
hint: Is there just one way to learn about new tools, or are there many ways?
explanation: You can find out about new cleaning tools and equipment by searching the internet, attending exhibitions and fairs, and reading newspapers and magazines. Use all available ways to keep learning and improving.
